title: run container-diff tests in Travis
body:
This commit will cause Travis to spawn a fedora container
which will build a Dockerfile using docker and buildah bud
and then compare both images using container-diff

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>


I'm thinking of moving the stuff in `.travis.yml` to the Fedora container, but that's for later.

@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat @mrunalp PTAL

comments.body: also, let me know if we have a good Dockerfile to use for this and what kind of container-diff tests are needed

On Tue, Sep 11, 2018 at 04:06:16PM -0700, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> TomSweeneyRedHat commented on this pull request.
> 
> 
> 
> > @@ -68,3 +80,11 @@ script:
>      - go test -c -tags "apparmor seccomp `./btrfs_tag.sh` `./libdm_tag.sh` `./ostree_tag.sh` `./selinux_tag.sh`" ./cmd/buildah
>      - tmp=`mktemp -d`; mkdir $tmp/root $tmp/runroot; sudo PATH="$PATH" ./buildah.test -test.v -root $tmp/root -runroot $tmp/runroot -storage-driver vfs -signature-policy `pwd`/tests/policy.json -registries-conf `pwd`/tests/registries.conf
>      - cd tests; travis_wait 60 sudo PATH="$PATH" ./test_runner.sh
> +    - cd ..
> +    - echo "Run container-diff tests in Fedora container..."
> +    - echo "Travis/host environment:"
> +    - env
> +    - echo
> +    - echo "Running tests in SPC using ./hack/run_build.sh"
> +    - "./hack/run_build.sh"
> +
> 
> Might be nice to do './hack/run_build.sh Dockerfile.nginx Dockerfile.OnBuild Dockerfile.withScript' or something along those lines so you wouldn't be limited to just one Dockerfile, but could potentially test 3 or 4 times.
> 
> But again, not something for this review, just something for the back of your mind possible enhancement.

I'm cool with doing it in this PR itself once I have a list of
Dockerfiles to use. But if you feel it's better off as future work, so be it.

-- 
Lokesh
IRC: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5

On Wed, Sep 12, 2018 at 05:29:51AM -0700, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> TomSweeneyRedHat commented on this pull request.
> 
> 
> 
> > @@ -68,3 +80,11 @@ script:
>      - go test -c -tags "apparmor seccomp `./btrfs_tag.sh` `./libdm_tag.sh` `./ostree_tag.sh` `./selinux_tag.sh`" ./cmd/buildah
>      - tmp=`mktemp -d`; mkdir $tmp/root $tmp/runroot; sudo PATH="$PATH" ./buildah.test -test.v -root $tmp/root -runroot $tmp/runroot -storage-driver vfs -signature-policy `pwd`/tests/policy.json -registries-conf `pwd`/tests/registries.conf
>      - cd tests; travis_wait 60 sudo PATH="$PATH" ./test_runner.sh
> 
> My only hesitancy with that move is the work I'm trying to pull off with the codecov.io.  If I ever manage to get that to work, the codecoverage file needs to be saved to a place that travis can get to it and then copy to codecov.io.   That's a lot easier without the tests running in a container, but is not insurmountable.  But then again, I'm not getting it to work for Buildah even with out a container at the moment.
> 
> So long story hopefully shortened, if it makes life easier for you to have them in a container rather than the top level travis, then go ahead and move them.
> 

Ack, alrighty, I'll leave this for later.

-- 
Lokesh
IRC: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5

On Wed, Sep 12, 2018 at 12:52:04PM +0000, Daniel J Walsh wrote:
> rhatdan commented on this pull request.
> 
> 
> 
> > @@ -68,3 +80,11 @@ script:
>      - go test -c -tags "apparmor seccomp `./btrfs_tag.sh` `./libdm_tag.sh` `./ostree_tag.sh` `./selinux_tag.sh`" ./cmd/buildah
>      - tmp=`mktemp -d`; mkdir $tmp/root $tmp/runroot; sudo PATH="$PATH" ./buildah.test -test.v -root $tmp/root -runroot $tmp/runroot -storage-driver vfs -signature-policy `pwd`/tests/policy.json -registries-conf `pwd`/tests/registries.conf
>      - cd tests; travis_wait 60 sudo PATH="$PATH" ./test_runner.sh
> +    - cd ..
> +    - echo "Run container-diff tests in Fedora container..."
> +    - echo "Travis/host environment:"
> +    - env
> +    - echo
> +    - echo "Running tests in SPC using ./hack/run_build.sh"
> +    - "./hack/run_build.sh"
> +
> 
> I think the script should take a single Dockerfile or none.  If None it uses the builtin.
> 
> If Dockerfile is specified then it runs with it.  
> 
> Then to do what Tom wants  you could just do
> 
> for i in "Dockerfile.nginx Dockerfile.OnBuild Dockerfile.withScript"; do ./hack/run_build.sh $i; done

SGTM, I'll leave it as-is for now in that case.
> 
> 
> > +          -v /run:/run -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime
> +          -v /var/log:/var/log -v /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup
> +          -v /var/lib/containers:/var/lib/containers
> +          -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
> +          --tmpfs /tmp:rw,nosuid,nodev,exec,relatime,mode=1777,size=50%
> +          --workdir $WORKDIR"
> +
> +ENV_ARGS="-e HOME=/root -e SHELL=/bin/bash -e SPC=true -e DISTRO=$DISTRO -e WORKDIR=$WORKDIR"
> +
> +echo
> +echo "Preparing to run \$SPCMD=$SPCCMD in a $DISTRO SPC."
> +echo "Override either for a different experience."
> +wait  # for backgrounded processes to finish
> +echo
> +set -x
> +sudo docker run -t $SPC_ARGS $VOL_ARGS $ENV_ARGS $TRAVIS_ENV $FQIN $SPCCMD
> 
> Why not use podman?
> 

The main reason here would be that it'd need me to get podman built for Ubuntu
14.04 :D . I can get that going, but would you be ok with that being
left for future work?

Also, the other issue is that within the Fedora container, container-diff assumes docker is running. I
haven't checked if podman-docker suffices for that.

-- 
Lokesh
IRC: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5
